title: Graduate Chemist
description:
RPS is looking for an Extraction Chemist to join our Laboratory Team based in Bedford. This position is ideally suited to a recent Graduate in Chemistry (or closely related subject) and will build your experience of working in a fast-paced, commercial laboratory environment with rapidly changing priorities.

This is a full time, permanent role working 5 days per week Monday - Sunday. Weekend working hours can be flexible dependant on business needs.

A s part of the Extraction Development scheme you can see your salary rise through regular performance reviews.

About The Team

RPS Laboratories is a team of Analysts, Chemists, and Technicians that operate from purpose-built laboratory facilities which have a long history of providing expert advice and analytical solutions to the water, utilities, agrochemical and defence sector. Our laboratories enjoy an international reputation for solving complex and novel analytical problems in a wide variety of food, feed, and forage crops.


 * Our Bedford laboratory was one of the first laboratories in the UK to gain UKAS accreditation for the analysis of PFAS chemicals following the monitoring required at the Buncefield oil terminal.
   
   

About You

As a key member of the Laboratories business, your role as a Extraction Chemist is critical to helping us conduct investigations and formulate effective solutions to help our clients comply with international quality standards, maximum residue limits (MRLs), environmental regulations, and much more.

By joining us you will collaborate with our clients, to design a tailored analytical programme to meet their complex regulatory needs. Leveraging our state-of-the-art instrumentation. You will be influential in providing analytical support during the remediation and clean-up project and subsequent routine monitoring.

Your Responsibilities


 * Follow established quality procedures to perform various types of sample extraction and preparation
 * Maintain a working stock of consumables and chemicals, which are essential to perform daily activities and tasks
 * Responsible for conducting a range of laboratory checks, including calibration and maintenance of laboratory equipment such as pipettes, balances and other bench tools
 * Work as part of a team to ensure that all other routine laboratory tasks are completed to ensure that the Laboratory operates smoothly
   
   

The Job May Involve The Following Possible Hazards


 * Manual handling
 * Handling chemicals and solvents
 * Handling samples that are potentially biohazardous
 * Handling of foods, including nuts
   
   

Skills, Knowledge, And Experience


 * Knowledge of basic laboratory skills and techniques, including use of pipettes, balances, syringes, titrations, dilutions etc
 * Good time management skills, attention to detail and ability to cope under pressure
 * Ability to follow written and verbal instructions, seeking advice where necessary
 * Understanding of Quality Control/Quality Assurance processes and their importance within a laboratory environment
 * Ability to work with minimal supervision as well as part of a team.
 * Good inter-personal and communication skills
   
   

What’s in it for you?

A personal development plan and a transparent career pathway puts you in the driving seat of your career and you’ll be supported as far as you want to go. A career here is far from ordinary. Here you’re not a number, you are part of the solution.

Why RPS?

We’re a connected community, working together to achieve exceptional outcomes. We understand the need to work flexibly, empowering our people to create a work-life balance that’s right for them. We also understand the importance of collaboration and social connection in the office environment. With agile offices offered as standard, we trust our people to find the right balance to meet client needs – this is a workplace that works for you.

About RPS, a Tetra Tech Company

RPS, part of Tetra Tech since January 2023, is a global firm that defines, designs, and manages projects in urbanisation, natural resources, and sustainability. As part of Tetra Tech’s 28,000-strong team across 550 offices in over 120 countries, we deliver solutions that create lasting value in an increasingly urbanised and resource-scarce world. By leveraging our global expertise, we enable our clients to develop winning solutions for their clients and communities.

As a Tetra Tech company, RPS is proud to provide market-leading development and project opportunities for our people, supporting their growth while addressing the challenges that matter. Our people drive our success, and this is where you come to build a career.

Take the next step in your career and help make our world a better and safer place.

What happens next?

If we feel you are a good match, we will be in touch to arrange a competency interview. If this opportunity suits your career journey, we’d love to hear from you. All applications will be considered. Ready to apply? Please have your CV ready and continue with your application online.

Recruitment Agencies We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Apprentice (GA)
description:
Please note: Due to the high level of interest in this role, a closing date will now be added to the advert. We'd encourage anyone interested to submit their application as soon as possible. The closing date is Friday 27th March.

We are seeking motivated and ambitious individuals to join us as Mechanical Engineering Graduate Apprentices (GA). You will join an engineering team delivering end‑to‑end solutions for marine handling and defence customers. You will design components and assemblies, complete engineering calculations, produce detailed drawings and technical documentation, and support prototype builds and testing. The role includes structured rotations across the business including Design, Test, Production and Support functions.

You will have 1-to-1 mentorship from experienced engineers and regular university‑workplace reviews to give you the support you need to fulfil your degree. You will have a 5-day working week with day release to the university and dedicated time to study whilst at work.

What You'll Gain


 * A degree - you will study towards a SCQF Level 10, Beng (Hons) in Engineering: Design & Manufacture (Mechanical) at Heriot Watt University for 4 years, which will be fully funded
 * Practical experience - Hands-on exposure to design analysis, CAD, manufacturing processes, testing, quality systems and international standards
 * Professional development - 1-to-1 mentoring, formal training, and opportunities to lead improvement projects that deliver measurable results
 * Career pathways - Potential specialisms in R&D, Design, Test, Bid Engineering, Electrical & Controls or Hydraulics and routes toward professional registration (IEng/CEng) through our established scheme.
   
   

Who This Would Suit


 * High school leavers - school leavers with SQA Highers or equivalent who want to start earning while studying for a degree and build practical engineering skills from day one
 * College or HNC graduates - candidates with vocational qualifications or HNCs seeking to progress to a full honours degree while applying existing workshop and technical experience in a professional setting
 * Career changers - people from other technical or non-technical backgrounds who are motivated to move into engineering and want a structured routed combining paid work, on-the-job training and university study
 * Hands-on problem solvers - those who enjoy designing, building and testing physical systems and want to see their ideas move from concept to finished item
 * Team players and communicators - individuals who can collaborate across engineering, production and commercial teams and who value clear, professional communication
 * Ambitions learners - people seeking a degree while earning and aiming for a long-term engineering career in defence or the marine sectors
 * Practical problem solvers who enjoy designing, building and testing physical systems
   
   

Requirements

What you'll need


 * Entry Qualifications - One of the following qualifications:
    * SQA Highers at BBBB including Mathematics plus Physics (or similar)
    * A-Levels at BBC including Mathematics plus Physics (or similar)
    * SQA HNC in an engineering discipline (or relevant Modern/Technical Apprenticeship) with Engineering Mathematics 1 & 2 (or equivalent)
    * Foundation Apprenticeship in Engineering alongside SQA Highers at BBB* including Mathematics
    * A combination of qualifications totalling 96 SCQF credits at Level 6 or minimum 106 UCAS points
    * Relevant experience will also be considered

 * Skills and attributes - Strong numeracy, attention to detail, curiosity, resilience and confident use of Microsoft Office. CAD experience is desirable
 * Other - The successful candidate must satisfy Ministry of Defence security requirements. You should also be happy to travel and work away when needed
   
   

Benefits

Salary and Benefits

The starting salary for this role is £21,826, with staged increases as you progress through the programme.

You will also benefit from:


 * Fully funded degree
 * Dedicated study time
 * Flexible working hours between 07:00 and 19:00, with core hours of 09:30 - 15:30
 * Competitive annual leave and family friendly policies
 * A supportive learning environment with a dedicated Learning & Development team
 * Access to an Employee Assistance Programme and comprehensive wellbeing support
 * Onsite facilities including canteen and free parking
 * Generous company pension scheme and life insurance
 * Health initiatives including flu vaccinations and sick pay support
 * On conclusion of your apprenticeship, choice of a 4- or 5-day working week to support a healthy work-life balance

title: Business & Science: Graduate Scheme
description:
Rentokil Pest Control - Graduate Pest Control Technician

Join Our Team and Make a Difference!

Enjoy problem-solving and helping customers? Become a Graduate Pest Control Technician with Rentokil. Full training provided, no experience necessary. Apply now!

We are currently seeking a Graduate Pest Control Technician to join our dedicated team covering Newport and surrounding area. Successful individuals in this role have included graduates with a science-based, business management or social science degree. If you're looking for a company that prioritises colleague development, this could be the job for you!

Why Join Rentokil?


 * Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £26,733 per annum
 * Expected OTE: £31,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available
 * Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards
 * Relocation Package: Moving from more than 2 hours away? We may offer up to £5,000 to help you settle in
 * Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary
 * Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers' pest control needs
   
   

Graduate Pest Control Technician Role:

You will start as a Pest Control Technician, with your initial 6-12 months dedicated to covering a specific geographical area. You'll provide invaluable support to customers by resolving their pest control issues while receiving comprehensive industry-recognised training. Key responsibilities include:


 * Inspecting customer premises for signs of pests and identifying potential risks
 * Swiftly tackling pest problems by cleaning and removing evidence of pest behaviour
 * Offering valuable advice to customers on the maintenance and hygiene of their premises
 * Completing accurate reports after every visit
 * Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times
   
   

During the next 12-36 months, you will have an exciting opportunity to choose your career path from one of the following roles:


 * Sales Surveyor
 * Field Biologist
 * Service Team Leader
 * Key Account Manager
   
   

Rentokil offers you the platform to follow your own career path while constantly learning on the job and developing your career.

Requirements


 * A minimum 2.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality or Tourism
 * Full UK manual driving licence with no more than 6 points
 * A desire to work hard and have fun while meeting and interacting with customers daily
 * Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs
 * You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for
   
   

Benefits


 * Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes
 * Access to a company vehicle and fuel card
 * Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business
 * Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme
 * Enrolment in our company pension scheme
 * Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards
 * Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers
 * Uncapped leads commission - although this isn't a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads
 * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends
 * Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service
 * Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial
   
   

A Company Putting "People First"

Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world's leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world's leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world's leading provider of plants and scenting.

As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.

Website

LinkedIn

Facebook

Instagram

Rentokil Initial are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data view our careers privacy policy here

description:

title: Graduate Recruitment and Development Coordinator
description:
Description

The Graduate Recruitment and Development Coordinator will deliver a full spectrum of Graduate Recruitment and Development (GR&D) activities as part of a best-in-class GR&D team, working closely with and providing first-class support to the GR&D Manager, GR&D Partner, and Graduate Recruitment panels and stakeholders. The role-holder will ensure first-class recruitment processes and an outstanding candidate experience designed to attract high-quality, diverse talent that supports the Firm’s Diversity and Inclusion strategy and strengthens the Firm’s market reputation as an employer of choice.

Main Responsibilities

Applicant Tracking System


 * Manage candidates through the recruitment process, including inviting to testing, video interviews, and assessment centers
 * Maintain housekeeping of the system and supporting the Graduate Recruitment Officer (GRO) in creating reports
   
   

Assessment Centers


 * Plan and coordinate assessment days; book rooms, coordinate interviewer and assessor availability, and distribute interview paperwork
 * Ensure that correspondence with candidates is timely
 * Ensure that all interview information is tracked and recorded
 * Work with and produce a regular report for the GRO and GR Partner on interview, offer, and acceptance statistics
   
   

Offer Process


 * Manage the training contract offer process, including drafting offer paperwork, uploading candidate information to the onboarding platform, maintaining all relevant trackers, drafting training contracts, and terms and conditions of employments for future Trainee Solicitors
   
   

Screening/Candidate Management


 * Screen training contract, vacation scheme, and open day applications in conjunction with the Graduate Recruitment & Development Manager (GRDM) and GRDOs
 * Support the GROs in reviewing video interviews for all programmes
   
   

Future Trainees


 * Support the GRDO with our future trainee process, including training contract offers, grants, fee payments and pastoral issues (supported by the GRDM)
 * Organize SQE events and future Trainee Solicitor social events
 * Liaise with BARBRI on all SQE-related onboarding and administration
 * Support future Trainee Solicitors to book SQE 1 and 2 exams
   
   

Inclusion & Diversity


 * Support the GRDM and the Inclusion and Diversity team in ensuring best practice aligned to our inclusion and diversity strategy
 * Support diversity events alongside the GRDO & GRDM
   
   

New Trainee Joiner Process


 * Set up onboarding for Trainee Solicitors joining the Firm in March and September
 * Create system profiles for the Trainee Solicitors
 * Coordinate IT and other new joiner requirements for Trainee Solicitors
   
   

Trainee Solicitor Development


 * Assist with logistical arrangements for Trainee solicitor seat moves, including all system updates on HR systems
 * Provide support for the qualification process, including collation of evaluation forms and final seat feedback, creating and updating Trainee Solicitor summary sheets, qualification presentation, and managing the qualification secure intranet site
 * Manage the NQ offer process, including system updates, leaver process, and related SRA administration
 * Support the GRO in the secondment process, including assistance with secondment agreements, guides, and benefit changes
   
   

Other Responsibilities


 * Support the GRO in the attraction, recruitment, and implementation of the early careers outreach programme
 * Ensure that all recruitment processes and communications support the Firm’s brand and provide a world-class candidate experience
 * Ensure that recruitment activities and initiatives are aligned to the Diversity & Inclusion (D&I) strategy of the Firm and that D&I is embedded in all processes
 * Support the implementation of innovative strategies to attract diverse candidates
 * Build strong relationships with internal stakeholders, including the Graduate Recruitment Partner, Graduate Recruitment Panel, Trainee Solicitors, Associates, and key business services teams
 * Participate in assigned recruitment work-streams and sub-streams as required
 * Provide support, as and when required, to the Recruitment Leadership Team and work collaboratively as part of a regional Recruitment team, assisting other offices on recruitment activity and projects
 * Participate in various recruiter organisations to increase networking, knowledge sharing, and promotion of the Firm
 * Attend ad-hoc events and presentations as needed
 * Participate in ad-hoc project work as required
 * Share responsibility for the Graduate Recruitment inbox, drafting responses using template text and escalating any complex queries as appropriate
 * Produce standard recruitment reports and maintain master documents
 * Process applicant and Trainee/future Trainee Solicitor reference requests
   
   

Skills And Experience


 * Experience in a similar role within a professional services organization at coordinator level
 * Conscientious, flexible, and with a high level of attention to detail
 * Proficient in using Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook
 * First class written and oral communication skills, with strong interpersonal skills
 * Able to maintain a calm, professional manner at all times, even when under pressure; acts as an ambassador for the Function and the Firm
 * Strong organizational skills and able to meet deadlines and multi-task in a fast-paced, matrix professional services environment
 * Able to plan, schedule, and arrange own activities to accomplish objectives
 * Strong client service and delivery mind-set
 * Able to work collaboratively and build effective networks across all levels of the business
   
   

Reports To

Graduate Recruitment & Development Manager

Travel Requirements

This role requires travel only within the United Kingdom

Position Type

In Market

Development Framework

Business Support

At Baker McKenzie we welcome direct applications.

We encourage the best people - regardless of race, religion or belief if any, gender, gender identity, disability, sexual orientation or age - to fulfill their professional aspirations with us. We are committed to ensuring an inclusive and accessible experience for all candidates.

title: Management Trainee - London
description:
At SIXT, we don't just hire people - we build leadership careers. 1ST GEAR! is our global Management Trainee Program - a dynamic, 12-month journey designed to fast-track your way into a real leadership role. From day one, you’ll gain hands-on experience, take on responsibility, and develop the skills you need to lead in one of the world’s most innovative mobility companies. What makes this program unique? You don’t just complete it - you graduate into a leadership position. Whether it’s managing your own downtown branch or becoming part of a high-performing airport leadership team, your next career step is guaranteed.

We’re looking for ambitious, curious, and proactive individuals who are ready to roll up their sleeves and grow fast. If you want to make an impact, lead with confidence, and shape the future of mobility, 1ST GEAR! is your starting line.



YOUR MANAGEMENT CAREER PATH AT SIXT:


 * You take on responsibility early in your 12-month program, support the achievement of our operational goals, and contribute to greater efficiency and shared success within the team. Upon successful completion of the program, you will be placed directly in a leadership position anywhere in the United Kingdom
 * You gain insights into key business areas such as Sales, Customer Service, Fleet Management, and Performance Management, and develop further along clear learning and performance goals
 * You work in the operational day-to-day business from day one, get to know our core operations as a Sales Executive, and are supported through training in communication, conflict resolution, sales, and problem-solving strategies
 * You collaborate closely with Branch Managers and Area Directors to learn leadership on the job
   
   
   



YOUR SKILLS MATTER


 * Growth Mindset You have completed a bachelor's or business management degree or gained relevant professional experience in Sales, Customer Service, Tourism, Hospitality and Management
 * Leadership and Entrepreneurial Potential You have a hands-on, entrepreneurial mindset, driven to lead - you don’t wait for instructions, you take the lead
 * Communication Excellence You have strong communication skills in English (other languages are a plus)
 * Proactive Attitude You work independently, solve problems, and take ownership
 * Agile Flexibility You are open to rotating across different locations and branches, you may also be asked to relocate in the UK
 * Mobility Readiness You hold a valid driver’s license and have at least 1 year of driving experience and 6 points or less
   
   
   



WHAT WE OFFER


 * Attractive Compensation Package Competitive base salary of £32,330 plus a premium company car from our fleet
 * Exclusive Employee Benefits From discounted rentals and premium uniforms to in-service life assurance, support through the Employee Assistance Programme, an enhanced family friendly policy and the Yu Life wellbeing APP
 * Generous Time Off Enjoy 22 days of annual leave plus bank holidays
 * Career Mobility & International Growth Opportunities across functions, cities, and even countries
 * Fast-track Leadership Development A structured 12-month program that sets you up for your first management role
   
   
   

Important to note:

If your application is successful, you must be available to attend an Assessment Centre on Wednesday 15th April in Slough. The program start date will be 1st June. You may also be required to relocate within the UK upon successful completion of the program.



About us:

We are a globally leading mobility service provider with a revenue of €4.00 billion and around 9,000 employees worldwide. Our mobility platform ONE combines our products SIXT rent (car rental), SIXT share (car sharing), SIXT ride (taxi, ride, and chauffeur services), and SIXT+ (car subscription), giving our customers access to our fleet of 350,000 vehicles, the services of 4,000 cooperation partners, and around 5 million drivers worldwide. Together with our franchise partners, we are present in more than 110 countries at 2,000 rental stations. At SIXT, top-tier customer experience and outstanding customer service are our highest priorities. We believe in true entrepreneurship and long-term stability and align our corporate strategy with foresight. Get started with us and apply now!

title: Graduate Sales Development Representative
description:
Feefo helps both consumers and businesses make the right decisions.

Founded in 2010, Feefo works with 6,000+ brands worldwide to collect reliable and constructive reviews they can learn from and display. We invite confirmed customers to leave feedback, which results in reliable, fake-free reviews, so consumers can learn how people like them feel about different products and services. And companies can discover what they’re doing right, and where they can improve. This allows Feefo’s clients to create transparent, trusted relationships and deliver exceptional services that their customers can depend on - every time.

We’re a team of technology specialists, industry experts, and multi-lingual client services champions that operates across various sectors, including travel, retail, automotive, and finance. Feefo’s bespoke artificial intelligence, business insight, review software and compliance solutions help increase client sales and reduce churn. As a Google Premier Partner, our clients can improve their search and paid conversion rates too.

We are proud to work with companies, large and small, from household names to local heroes.

To learn more visit: www.feefo.com, LinkedIn, and Twitter.

About The Role

The SDR will focus on outreach, prospecting, and lead qualification, connecting with and following

up on as many leads as possible and determining if they are good customer fits. The SDR will

generate and follow up on leads moving them effectively through the sales pipeline, connecting and

learning about businesses and needs. Once qualified, and a demo is booked, the SDR will be

responsible for smoothly transitioning the prospect to the Sales Consultant who will take over to

close the deal. Follow up on inbound leads and generate own leads through market research,

identifying potential companies to contact based on specific criteria.

What You'll Do


 * Identify key decision-makers
 * Build insight enabling you to engage and educate providing solutions to problems and challenges.

title: Sports Science Graduate Role
description:
Job Title: Occupational Health Technician

Contract: Permanent, Full-time (37.5 hours per week)

Salary: £26,325 per annum. This does not include overtime which can take earnings up to 33K per annum (calculated on an average of 8 – 9 hrs overtime per week)

Location: Guildford/Dorking




Are you a graduate in Sports Science, or similar field, ready to put your passion for health and performance into action?

At Medigold Health, we’re on the lookout for enthusiastic, proactive individuals to join our dynamic team as Occupational Health Technicians. This is a fantastic opportunity to kickstart your career in a growing and rewarding industry.




What Does the Role Involve?

As an Occupational Health Technician, you’ll be part of a team that helps organisations look after the health and wellbeing of their employees. You'll drive a Mobile Testing Unit to client sites to deliver a variety of health assessments that support early detection of health issues and promote long-term wellbeing at work.




Your day-to-day might include:

 * Carrying out health surveillance tests such as:

- Audiometry (hearing tests)

- Spirometry (lung function tests)

- Skin prick allergy testing

- Vision screening

- Venepuncture (blood draws) – we’ll give you full training

 * Preparing and maintaining equipment to ensure everything runs smoothly and safely
 * Identifying results that need follow-up and escalating them to our clinical team
 * Supporting health promotion initiatives, like wellness events or wellbeing campaigns
 * Helping to improve processes, ensuring we keep our service efficient, effective, and patient-focused
 * Completing clinical paperwork and updating health records accurately

No two days are quite the same. You’ll be working independently much of the time, so being confident, organised and self-motivated is key. Throughout your role, you’ll be supported by a friendly, experienced clinical team every step of the way.

This role is a great opportunity to get hands-on clinical experience, develop your skills, and build a solid foundation for a career in occupational health.




Who we're looking for?

This role is perfect for you if you:

 * Have a degree in Sports Science, Physiology, Health & Exercise Science, or a related field
 * Have a full clean UK driver’s licence, able to drive and park a mobile unit at home. A special licence is not required
 * Are a great communicator with excellent organisational skills
 * Are hands-on, motivated, and excited about improving health outcomes in the workplace
 * Want to build a career with purpose and progression

Bonus points if you have experience as an EMT, Occupational Health Technician, or in nursing, but it’s not essential. We provide full training!




Why Medigold Health?

We’re one of the UK’s leading Occupational Health providers, offering a supportive, forward-thinking environment where you can grow, thrive, and make a real difference.




Our Company Benefits

 * 31 days holiday inclusive of bank holidays, increasing with length of service
 * Pension Scheme
 * Access to Company benefits and discount portal
 * Access to a Health Cash Plan
 * Free eyecare vouchers
 * Cycle to work scheme
 * Access to confidential Employee Assistance programme
 * Interactive mental health and wellbeing app




Interested?

If you’d like to explore the role further or have any questions before applying, we’d be happy to chat. Get in touch at: recruitment@medigold-health.com.




Additional Information

Mobile Units:

 1. Candidates must have a full clean driver’s licence, able to drive and park a mobile unit at home.
 2. Mobile units are 3.5 tons, a special licence is not required.
 3. All MU drivers will be provided with driver familiarisation training.
 4. All drivers are required to undergo a Drivers Medical.




Salary and Experience:

 1. Employment with Medigold is subject to the individual undergoing a DBS check and evidence of Right to Work in the UK.
 2. Full training is provided to all candidates.




Working Area:

 1. Overnight stays will be required for this location. Hotel and meal allowances are paid by the company.




Working starts and finishes:

 1. Start and finish times are dependent on our client needs which can be a mixture of early, usual or late starts and some shift work between the hours of 11pm and 6am paid at a higher rate.
 2. Core hours are 37.5 hours per week (7.5 hours x 5 days per week).
 3. Overtime is usually made up of travel time to and from site and paid at a higher rate.

title: Graduate Scheme- Support technician
description:
All the opportunity, no commute.

The career you thought only existed in London – without the noise, chaos, commutes, or high rent.

No hierarchy, just growth.

Everyone starts at the same level. No senior hires. No tech background needed, just ambition and curiosity.

Side by side, not top down.

From Day One, you’ll sit with the people who run the business – and it’ll stay that way.

Work hard, pay hard.

Earn more and way faster than anyone else in your field. Your growth is entirely up to you.

Support Technician

Everyone at Halo starts here.

As a Support Technician, you’ll be the first point of contact for customers who need help using Halo. You’ll ask the right questions to understand the issue, investigate what’s happening, and guide customers through the solution.

At the start, you’ll work closely with experienced mentors who will support you through each case. As your confidence grows, you’ll begin solving issues more independently and building a deep understanding of how Halo works.

This role develops your technical skills, product knowledge, and problem-solving ability, forming the foundation for progression into other roles across the business.

And This Is How We Do It

Sports Team Mentality

We’re not a “family” – families are unconditional. We’re a high-performing sports team. We hire and develop people to become future stars and aren’t afraid to make changes if someone isn’t pulling their weight. You start in a cohort so you learn together, push each other, and raise the standard from day one. We all win together.

The Office Matters

Sure, we’re flexible, but if you’re not in the office, you’ll miss the spark that built Halo from the ground up. Collaboration is essential and it is to your benefit to come in every single day. At Halo, there has yet to be one person to receive external training. All knowledge is picked up from peers, and it’s the collaborative nature of our office that allows our best people to develop exceptionally fast.

Long-Term Impact

Every decision we make today is with the bigger picture in mind. We’re playing the long game, building something that lasts, and we want to work with people who share that vision.

Own Your Path

Don’t expect a manager to tell you what to do next. You’re trusted to figure that out. If that sounds daunting, that’s okay. It’s also exciting – because the only limit to what you can achieve here is your imagination.

Boundaries Don't Exist

You might find yourself collaborating across different teams, picking up new skills, and moving to wherever you can make the biggest impact. We don’t believe in rigid roles or fixed departments – flexibility is essential.

Travel

Some roles include travel to customer sites across the UK and occasionally abroad. We arrange and cover travel, pair you with experienced teammates, and make sure visits add real learning and impact.

Titles Don't Define You

The most senior leaders work shoulder-to-shoulder with new joiners. Because titles don’t matter – your contribution does. From Day One, your opinions and ideas are heard. You’ll have direct access to decision-makers, and your ideas can shape the future.

Where Work Meets Play

We give our all because we care, but we also know how to switch off. With our own bar, pool table, table tennis, and regular team events, work rarely feels like work. Everyone is at a similar stage in life, with shared passions and interests – the energy is simply contagious. It’s this collective drive that pushes us to perform at our best every day.

Support Technician

Everyone at Halo starts here.

As a Support Technician, you’ll be the first point of contact for customers who need help using Halo. You’ll ask the right questions to understand the issue, investigate what’s happening, and guide customers through the solution.

At the start, you’ll work closely with experienced mentors who will support you through each case. As your confidence grows, you’ll begin solving issues more independently and building a deep understanding of how Halo works.

This role develops your technical skills, product knowledge, and problem-solving ability, forming the foundation for progression into other roles across the business.

title: Graduate Enterprise IT Support Engineer - London
description:
At Acturis, innovation isn’t just part of our culture - it’s in our DNA.

Our story began in 2000 in the garden shed of our co‑founder, Theo Duchen, who, alongside fellow former McKinsey partner David McDonald, set out to reinvent insurance technology. What started as a bold start‑up idea has grown into a global SaaS leader trusted by the industry.

Today, over 100,000 users across 40+ countries rely on the Acturis platform. By working closely with brokers, insurers, and MGAs, we continue to shape the market and drive new standards across both personal and commercial lines.

Acturis is proud to be recognised as a Great Place to Work® 2026 and to maintain a collaborative, innovative, start‑up‑style culture, even as we scale globally. With over 90% of our hires coming from graduate backgrounds, this is a place built for early‑career talent to thrive. And as an EcoVadis Committed‑rated organisation, we focus on creating long‑term value by operating in a sustainable and responsible way.

Day To Day You Will


 * Work full time in central London providing first line support to colleagues.
 * Log and manage issues through our help desk system while supporting 800 colleagues globally.
 * Build and maintain a large fleet of laptops using technologies such as MDT, and follow documented IT procedures including onboarding, Active Directory checks, and office equipment setup.
   
   

We offer mentorship from day one, a clear progression path, and real responsibility early on, because we’ve built an environment where graduates grow fast and make meaningful impact.

You Can Expect

 * Opportunities to lead projects, manage resources, and mentor junior team members.
 * Intensive onboarding, industry-recognised training, and funded specialist courses as you progress.
   
   

Job Requirements

You'll thrive here if you:


 * Have (or expect) a 2:1+ in a STEM degree and strong A-levels (AAB minimum, including A in Maths).
 * Are analytical, organised, and quick to learn new concepts.
 * Demonstrable passion for programming and experience working with an object-orientated programming language such as, C#, Java, C++, or Python
   
   

Ready to apply your technical mind to real‑world challenges?

Join Acturis and build a career where you’ll grow quickly, innovate boldly, and work alongside some of the brightest people in the industry.

Please note Acturis is an equal opportunities employer and positively encourages applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, transgender status, religion or belief, marital status, or pregnancy and maternity. title: Graduate Operations Manager
description:
Department: Graduate Recruitment

Location: Brunel House, Derby

Compensation: £30,000 / year



Description

Launch your career and become a future leader with our graduate programme at Network Plus!
We recruit high-calibre, motivated graduates and engage with you before you finish University so you can step straight into a graduate role from September 2026, whilst also completing our two-year structured development programme, designed to build and develop your leadership capability whilst also developing specialist skills in your chosen field.

Why choose Network Plus

Network Plus is an award-winning business delivering essential utility and infrastructure services for the UK’s major providers of gas, power, telecoms, transport, water, and wastewater.
We value the variety of experience, perspective, and other points of difference our workforce, clients, and supply chain offer.
We are actively working with colleagues across the Network Plus Group to develop an inclusive environment – we want all our employees to feel valued and included to enable everyone to thrive at work and understand the value of their contribution matters no matter their background, identity, or circumstances.
You’ll complete structured placements across different contracts and business areas, alongside a graduate cohort at the same stage of learning—making rotations and swaps straightforward and impactful and building a strong graduate community.

As a Graduate Operations Manager, you’ll step straight into a high‑impact role where you’ll learn to lead the operational and financial performance of a contract driving safe, on‑time delivery that meets and exceeds customer expectations. As an integral part of our contract succession plan, you’ll learn from experienced Operations Managers across the business, building the skills and ambition to become a future leader.

What we offer


Support and Development
 * Dedicated mentor support from our senior management team to guide your journey
 * Leadership development and cohort-based learning resulting in a recognized qualification
 * 360 feedback, coaching and continuous support throughout rotations

Real Impact and Challenge
 * Take part in a Business Sustainability challenge, tackling real-world issues and presenting solutions at an executive level
 * A strong graduate community with peer cohorts where you will grow together throughout the programme

Community
 * Professional skills, confidence and experience that set you up for long‑term success

What you’ll be doing
Across your two-year programme you’ll complete four 6-month rotations in different parts of the business, giving you the breadth and depth needed to understand how Network Plus thrives and where your strengths truly lie.
During each rotation, you’ll take on real responsibilities from day one, contributing to live projects and working alongside experienced colleagues who will support your development.


Key Responsibilities


 * Maintaining contract compliance with the dig crews
 * Chairing the daily Toughbook compliance meeting around redlines and offsite forms
 * Reimplementing the team dashboard that measures compliance information such as productivity, GCat compliance speeding etc
 * Taking control of the BW contract fleet, plant, logistics. Ensuring all vehicles are allocated to the correct person and regular plant audits are undertaken
 * Taking lead of the depots and maintaining compliance with H&S, inductions and delivery procedures.
 * Ensuring that the logistics manager has the relevant support to keep consumable items stocked, including emergency OOH stock
 * Finalising and setting up a dedicated training/ induction facility within the yard
 * Becoming an expert in the company and contract induction process
 * Being responsible for the daily jeopardy exercise which confirms what dig work has been completed, what needs reallocating and what is at risk of cancellation to maximise dig crew efficiency
   
   
   

Skills, Knowledge and Expertise

• Curious, driven graduates with a collaborative mindset, a willingness to learn and a desire to make an impact
• Final‑year students or recent graduates with a role‑related degree (2:2 or above)
• Full UK driving licence (some travel during placements)
• Individuals who want responsibility early and enjoy learning by doing
• Must have graduated within the last two years
• Critical thinking and problem solving
• Proficient IT skills, including Microsoft Excel and Word


Salary and Benefits

We offer a competitive salary based on experience along with a full benefits package.
Network Plus is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, nationality, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.

We are Armed Forces-friendly. We welcome applications from ex-Armed Forces personnel, reservists, armed forces veterans, cadet instructors and military spouses/partners.

We understand that privacy and the security of your personal information is extremely important. By applying for this role, you agree to the terms of our privacy policy.

 * Tuesday: 08:45 – 17:30
 * Friday: 08:45 – 16:30

Whether you’re finishing college, graduating from university or looking for a career change – there’s more than one way to begin your career with us in accountancy, audit or tax. Our trainee roles are designed to help you learn on the job, gain a professional qualification, and build a future you can be proud of, whichever path you take to get there.

We’re now inviting applications for our Early Careers Trainee Programme, designed for those ready to take their first step into a rewarding career in accountancy. Whether your passion lies in Audit, Accounts or Tax, you’ll gain the skills, qualifications and experience to become a fully qualified professional, supported every step of the way.

About Us

In 2024, DPC became part of Sumer Group – a collaboration of renowned regional accountancy practices with a shared vision to champion local small to medium-sized enterprises.

By bringing together the best in advisory & business services, Sumer Group retains the value that community-based practices offer and combines this with the scale, breadth of expertise and technologies that only a national organisation can muster.

Through our unique approach, we believe we can make a real positive impact and achieve growth for our company, our colleagues and our clients. Together, we are about doing the right things, putting people first and delivering the best possible services to those who sit at the heart of our economy and communities.

You’ll be joining a friendly, professional team where enthusiasm, curiosity and commitment are valued as highly as experience. From day one, you’ll work with a range of clients — learning how to prepare accounts, support audits, and provide insightful tax and business advice.

As your confidence grows, so will your responsibilities. You’ll have opportunities to work directly with clients, contribute to projects, and gain a real understanding of how successful businesses operate.

We’ll equip you with the technical training and personal skills development you need to progress, ensuring you build a strong foundation for a long-term career in accountancy.



The Programme

You’ll join a training contract, combining practical client experience with professional study towards your qualification (AAT, ACA, ACCA, ATT or CTA).

Alongside your qualification, you’ll become part of the Sumer Academy – our dedicated early careers development programme. The Academy runs alongside your studies, offering a blend of online, virtual and in-person learning focused on building both your technical skills and the soft skills that will help you thrive in your future career.

You’ll also train as part of a group-wide cohort, connecting with other trainees from across the Sumer Group to share experiences and learn together.

To apply, you’ll need a strong A-Level or equivalent academic background, including a minimum of a Grade C/5 in English and B/6 Maths at GCSE level (or equivalent).

You’ll also be:
 * An effective communicator – confident in listening and expressing ideas clearly with clients and colleagues.
 * Collaborative – a team player who enjoys sharing ideas and working together to achieve great results.
 * Time efficient – able to balance work and study with good organisation and prioritisation.
 * A problem solver – curious, adaptable and ready to take on challenges.
 * Results focused – proud to deliver a high standard of work and service.
 * Keen to learn – enthusiastic about developing your skills and career.

Applications will close on 24th March at 5pm. If your application is shortlisted you will receive a link to our online assessment via our partner provider, Canditech, around the 25th March where you will have 7 days to complete this.

The final stage is our in person assessment centre, which will take place on the 8th April in our office in Stoke on Trent.

Key Details


• Locations: Stoke on Trent
• Contract Type: Permanent
• Hours: 37.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday
• Start Date: August 2026

Belong @ Sumer

At Sumer, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment, not just as buzzwords, but as the heartbeat of our team. Our recruitment practices reflect this commitment, welcoming individuals from all backgrounds. We prioritise creating an environment where every candidate feels respected and supported throughout the hiring process.

Everyone is unique and we understand that each individual may have distinct needs to showcase their best selves during interviews. Our commitment is to offer personalised support for all candidates. Let our team know how we can bring out your fullest potential.

Our Values


We have a strong set of values that define who we are and everything we do:
 * WE SHINE TOGETHER – We celebrate everyone’s individuality, and work as one team.
 * WE DO THE RIGHT THING – We act with kindness, fairness and integrity in everything we do.
 * WE MAKE IT COUNT – We’re quietly brave as we make a difference, every day, for our clients and communities.
   
   

What's On Offer

• Full study support, including paid study leave and course fees
• Buddy and mentoring systems to help you settle in and progress
• Group-wide cohort training through the Sumer Academy
• Opportunities to grow your career beyond qualification
• Exposure to a diverse range of clients and projects

title: Business and Science Graduate Scheme
description:
Rentokil Pest Control - Graduate Pest Control Technician

Join Our Team and Make a Difference!

Enjoy problem-solving and helping customers? Become a Graduate Pest Control Technician with Rentokil in Gloucester and the surrounding areas. Full training provided, no experience necessary. Apply now!

We are currently seeking a Graduate Pest Control Technician to join our dedicated team covering Gloucester. Successful individuals in this role have included graduates with a science-based, business management or social science degree. If you're looking for a company that prioritises colleague development, this could be the job for you!

Why Join Rentokil?


 * Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £26733
 * Expected OTE: £31,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available
 * Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards
 * Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary
 * Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers' pest control needs
   
   

Graduate Pest Control Technician Role:

You will start as a Pest Control Technician, with your initial 6-12 months dedicated to covering a specific geographical area. You'll provide invaluable support to customers by resolving their pest control issues while receiving comprehensive industry-recognised training. Key responsibilities include:


 * Inspecting customer premises for signs of pests and identifying potential risks
 * Swiftly tackling pest problems by cleaning and removing evidence of pest behaviour
 * Offering valuable advice to customers on the maintenance and hygiene of their premises
 * Completing accurate reports after every visit
 * Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times
   
   

During the next 12-36 months, you will have an exciting opportunity to choose your career path from one of the following roles:


 * Sales Surveyor
 * Field Biologist
 * Service Team Leader
 * Key Account Manager
   
   

Rentokil offers you the platform to follow your own career path while constantly learning on the job and developing your career.

Requirements


 * A minimum 2.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality or Tourism
 * A desire to work hard and have fun while meeting and interacting with customers daily
 * Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs
 * You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for
   
   

Benefits


 * Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes
 * Access to a company vehicle and fuel card
 * Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business
 * Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme
 * Enrolment in our company pension scheme
 * Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards
 * Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers
 * leads commission - although this isn't a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads
 * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends
 * Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service
 * Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial
   
   

A Company Putting "People First"

Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world's leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world's leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world's leading provider of plants and scenting.

As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.

Our Social Links

Website

LinkedIn

Facebook

Instagram

Rentokil Initial are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data view our careers privacy policy here

title: Graduate Employment Advisor
description:
Job Introduction

Get Set for Your First Big Impact: Join Us as a Graduate Employment Advisor

Location: Portsmouth

Contract: FT 35hrs PW M-F

Salary: £24 - 26k DOE

Role Purpose

You’ll support people facing barriers to work by coaching them through confidence-building, skills development, and practical job search - helping them take positive steps toward employment.

Success in this role looks like

Participants making measurable progress, securing and sustaining work, and feeling supported and empowered at every stage of their journey.

Your Role: What You’ll Achieve

At Get Set UK, we believe in giving people the tools and support to unlock their potential - and that includes our own team. As a Graduate Employment Advisor, you'll start your career with purpose, supporting jobseekers into meaningful employment while gaining real-world coaching, commercial and sector experience.

This is your chance to build skills that matter, in communication, coaching, organisation, and problem-solving, all while helping others progress into work and independence. You'll be part of a collaborative, mission-led team that's driven to make a difference, one person at a time.

In this role, you'll help jobseekers build confidence, identify opportunities, and move forward with support. You'll gain hands-on experience in employment support, while being fully trained, guided, and developed by experienced colleagues.

You'll learn how to


 * Strategic Direction: Understand programme goals and structure your day around real outcomes for real people.
 * Deliver Results: Help participants become job-ready with CV support, job search guidance, and interview preparation.
 * People & Communication: Build rapport with participants, coach them through change, and communicate clearly with employers.
 * Get It Right: Keep accurate records, follow safeguarding processes, and stay on top of tasks and updates.
 * Business Acumen: Learn about local labour markets and contribute to connecting people with the right roles.
   
   

This role is ideal for a graduate who wants to build a meaningful career in employment support, recruitment, coaching, or social impact - and who's ready to take that first step with support and structure behind them.

Who You Are

You're ready to start your career with purpose. You might be a recent graduate or someone early in your professional journey, but you're motivated, people-focused, and eager to learn. You show up on time, communicate clearly, and take pride in helping others succeed. You understand that our customers expect us to model employability, so you lead by example.

Specifically, you bring


 * A degree in any subject (or equivalent practical experience)
 * Strong communication skills - written, verbal, and interpersonal
 * Curiosity and drive - you love learning, problem-solving, and challenging targets.
 * A proactive mindset - you take responsibility for your own development
 * Empathy and resilience - you support people through challenges and change
   
   

No experience in employment support? No problem. We'll train and support you - what matters most is your attitude, potential, and values.

You Share Our Values

We Live Our Values Every Day, In Every Role


 * Be Ambitious: We act with drive and purpose
 * Be Inspiring: We empower and uplift those around us
 * Be Proud: We take ownership and celebrate our success
 * Be Considerate: We collaborate and respect others
 * Own it: We show up and do what’s needed
   
   

Our customers count on us...and we count on you! That means showing up, being present, communicating clearly, and doing what you say you will. If it sounds like you, you'll thrive here. If it doesn't feel like the right fit, that's ok too, we'd rather help you save time than take the wrong step.

Get Set for More: Our Benefits

You’ll benefit from a supportive, progressive environment where learning is part of every role.


 * Immediate access to wellbeing tools (GP, counselling, Headspace)
 * Enhanced healthcare and lifestyle rewards with service
 * Discounts, pension scheme, and salary sacrifice options
 * Structured development through our Your Career, Your Journey framework
 * Internal Learning & Development team embedding growth in every role
   
   

What to Know Before You Apply


 * We reserve the right to close this advert early, so apply soon to avoid disappointment.
 * We’re a Disability Confident Employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. If you need any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, just let us know, we’re committed to supporting you.
 * This role is subject to our safeguarding and vetting process.
 * We do not accept unsolicited CVs from agencies.
   
   

Ready to Get Set?

If you’re excited by this opportunity and believe you can make a difference, apply and help us shape futures…starting with yours!

title: Management Trainee - Aberdeen
description:

What makes this program unique? You don’t just complete it - you graduate into a leadership position. Whether it’s managing your own downtown branch or becoming part of a high-performing airport leadership team, your next career step is guaranteed.

We’re looking for ambitious, curious, and proactive individuals who are ready to roll up their sleeves and grow fast. If you want to make an impact, lead with confidence, and shape the future of mobility, 1ST GEAR! is your starting line.



YOUR MANAGEMENT CAREER PATH AT SIXT:


 * You take on responsibility early in your 12-month program, support the achievement of our operational goals, and contribute to greater efficiency and shared success within the team. Upon successful completion of the program, you will be placed directly in a leadership position anywhere in the United Kingdom
 * You gain insights into key business areas such as Sales, Customer Service, Fleet Management, and Performance Management, and develop further along clear learning and performance goals

YOUR SKILLS MATTER


 * Growth Mindset You have completed a bachelor's or business management degree or gained relevant professional experience in Sales, Customer Service, Tourism, Hospitality and Management
 * Leadership and Entrepreneurial Potential You have a hands-on, entrepreneurial mindset, driven to lead - you don’t wait for instructions, you take the lead

 * Proactive Attitude You work independently, solve problems, and take ownership
 * Agile Flexibility You are open to rotating across different locations and branches, you may also be asked to relocate in the UK

WHAT WE OFFER

Important to note:

If your application is successful, you must be available to attend an Assessment Centre on Wednesday 15th April in Slough. The program start date will be 1st June. You may also be required to relocate within the UK upon successful completion of the program.



About us:

Our mobility platform ONE combines our products SIXT rent (car rental), SIXT share (car sharing), SIXT ride (taxi, ride, and chauffeur services), and SIXT+ (car subscription), giving our customers access to our fleet of 350,000 vehicles, the services of 4,000 cooperation partners, and around 5 million drivers worldwide. Together with our franchise partners, we are present in more than 110 countries at 2,000 rental stations. At SIXT, top-tier customer experience and outstanding customer service are our highest priorities. We believe in true entrepreneurship and long-term stability and align our corporate strategy with foresight. Get started with us and apply now!

title: Windows Enterprise Graduate
description:
LMAX Group is a global financial technology company and the leading independent operator of multiple institutional execution venues for FX and cryptocurrency trading. The Group’s portfolio includes LMAX Exchange (institutional FX exchange and FCA regulated MTF), LMAX Global (FCA and CySec regulated brokers), and LMAX Digital (GFSC regulated institutional spot cryptocurrency exchange).

Our mission is to create a level playing field for all participants in the world’s biggest financial markets, through transparent, precise and consistent execution, based on robust trading technology.

We are looking for people who want to help us change the game, not just play it.

LMAX Group fosters inclusive recruitment practices. We are an equal opportunity employer as we welcome everyone to our team. We encourage people of colour, women, people from LGBTQ+ community, and people with disabilities to apply. Please let us know if you need reasonable adjustments at any point in the application process.

This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious graduate to gain 12 months of intensive, hands-on experience at a leading global FinTech firm. As a Graduate Operations Analyst (1-Year FTC), you will immediately contribute to our global institutional Platform across FX and Digital Assets.

This role is a defined, entry-level contract designed to provide you with high-value IT, commercial and financial markets experience in a regulated, institutional environment. You will be an integral part of the Operations team, providing essential support and directly impacting our IT operations. This position is ideal for candidates looking for a focused, fast-paced IT foundation before pursuing long-term career goals.

Your Responsibilities


 * Proactive Technical Support: Investigating, troubleshooting, and resolving alerts across Microsoft-based applications and operating systems.
 * Incident Management & Triage: Monitoring system alerts and performing root-cause analysis to ensure high system availability.
 * Documentation & Knowledge Sharing: Contributing to the internal knowledge base by documenting troubleshooting steps and system configurations to improve team efficiency.
 * Hardware & Infrastructure: Assisting with the configuration, installation, and deployment of workstations for the LMAX team.
 * Security & Compliance: Contributing to the hardening and security of our diverse Windows and macOS environments.
 * Service Excellence: Managing day-to-day operational queries from internal teams, ensuring a high level of service and professional communication.
 * Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partnering with the Service Desk, Network, Security, and Systems teams to streamline workflows and implement long-term technical fixes.
   
   

Requirements

We are looking for analytical thinkers who are passionate about the intersection of technology and finance.


 * Academic & Technical Background:
 * Education: A minimum 2:1 undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Computing, Engineering, Software, STEM, or a related field.
 * System Administration: Hands-on experience managing computer systems (home-lab or professional). Proficiency in Microsoft & macOS is preferred.
 * Analytical Problem Solving: Strong diagnostic skills; the ability to break down complex technical problems into logical, manageable components.
   
   

Core Competencies


 * Growth Mindset: A self-starter attitude with a commitment to continuous learning and staying abreast of emerging technologies.
 * Communication: The ability to articulate complex technical concepts clearly and confidently to various stakeholders.
 * Precision & Adaptability: A meticulous approach to data handling and the ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.
 * Cultural Alignment: A natural resonance with our core values of transparency, fairness, and precision.
   
   

Benefits


 * 25 days of holiday
 * Bonus
 * Pension contribution
 * Private medical, dental, and vision coverage
 * Life assurance
 * Critical illness cover
 * Wellness contribution program with access to ClassPass
 * Plumm Platform
 * Five volunteering days
 * Give as You Earn initiative
 * Learning and development programs
 * Electric Vehicle Scheme
 * Cycle to Work Scheme
 * Season Ticket Loan

title: SITE ENGINEER GRADUATE

 * Company Vehicle with Fuel Card.
 * Marriage Leave
 * Enhanced Family Friendly Benefits

title: Trainee Recruiter
description:
Trainee Recruitment Consultant

TechChain Talent, Chiswick, London | Full-Time, On-Site




📍 Location: Chiswick, London (5 days on-site) 💷 Salary: £30,000 base + uncapped commission

📋 Contract: Permanent, Full-Time 🎓 Experience: No previous recruitment experience required




About TechChain Talent

TechChain Talent is one of the world's leading specialist recruitment agencies dedicated exclusively to the blockchain, Web3, and crypto ecosystem. We partner with some of the most recognised and fastest-growing projects in the industry, from Layer 1 and Layer 2 protocol teams to DeFi platforms, digital asset banks, and infrastructure providers.

Our client roster includes globally recognised names across the blockchain space. We place top-tier talent into technical, commercial, legal, and operations roles with reach across Europe, the US, and beyond.

Based in Chiswick, West London, our team operates with a boutique mentality and a global mindset. We move fast, operate with integrity, and genuinely care about the careers we help shape.




The Opportunity

This is a rare chance to enter the world of specialist technology recruitment from the ground up inside one of the most exciting sectors in the global economy.

As a Trainee Recruitment Consultant, you will be fully trained and mentored from day one. There is no prior recruitment experience required and we genuinely welcome applications from school leavers, recent graduates, and career changers alike. What matters most is your attitude, your hunger to succeed, and your curiosity about the world of blockchain and crypto.

You will join a close-knit, high-performing team and be given the tools, training, and support to build a successful desk within our growing business.




Key Responsibilities

Candidate Sourcing & Engagement

 * Source exceptional candidates through LinkedIn Recruiter, job boards, referrals, and direct headhunting
 * Write compelling outreach messages and job adverts that attract high-quality talent
 * Conduct structured screening interviews to assess candidate suitability, motivations, and career goals
 * Manage candidate pipelines from initial contact through to offer and placement

Process & Administration

 * Maintain accurate and up-to-date records within our CRM (Manatal)
 * Coordinate interview scheduling and provide timely feedback to both clients and candidates
 * Prepare candidate summaries and present shortlists to clients in a clear, professional format
 * Support senior consultants on live assignments and contribute to team targets

Business Development & Client Management

 * Research, identify, and approach new client companies across the blockchain and Web3 ecosystem
 * Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with hiring managers and HR teams at leading crypto firms
 * Represent TechChain Talent professionally across all client interaction: phone, email, LinkedIn, and in person
 * Develop a deep understanding of client cultures, hiring needs, and the competitive landscape




Who We're Looking For

We're interested in people, not just CVs. We don't require a specific degree or prior work background, we're looking for individuals who are:

 * Ambitious and motivated by results, you want to build something and earn well doing it
 * Great communicators, you're confident on the phone, clear in writing, and good with people
 * Naturally curious, you're interested in technology, finance, or the world of crypto and Web3
 * Resilient, you can handle rejection, stay positive under pressure, and keep going
 * Organised, you can manage multiple tasks and priorities without dropping the ball
 * A team player, you thrive in a collaborative, fast-paced environment

Backgrounds we actively encourage to apply include:

 * School leavers (A Levels or equivalent)
 * University graduates (any discipline)
 * Career changers from sales, science, sport, finance, law, or any other background
 * Those with some prior commercial or customer-facing experience




What We Offer

 * £30,000 base salary with an uncapped, transparent commission structure
 * Comprehensive on-the-job training and mentorship from day one
 * Fast-track progression - strong performers can reach Senior Consultant level within 12–18 months
 * Exposure to globally recognised clients at the frontier of the blockchain industry
 * A dynamic, non-corporate culture where performance is recognised and rewarded
 * A central Chiswick office with excellent transport links
 * Team incentives, lunches, socials and international conferences




Equal Opportunities

TechChain Talent is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment and welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences.
sector: Blockchain Services and Staffing and Recruiting

title: M&A Origination Analyst
description:
We are seeking a commercially minded, proactive and determined individual to join our M&A Origination team. 




We are looking for someone who relishes the early stages of business development, as well as supporting the teams deal pipeline.

 * The proactive identification and assessment of potential acquisition targets

What you'll need;

 * Knowledge of M&A & deal origination/business development is desirable
 * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

To be considered you must be a graduate or equivalent with strong interpersonal skills. You will be a motivated, self-starter and is an effective team player. If you have existing experience in M&A or lead generation we would love to hear from you!

title: Graduate Technical Engineer

description:
MCS Group are working with a well-established engineering and manufacturing organisation to recruit a Technical Engineer to join their team based in the UK.

This organisation operates within a fast-paced, customer-focused environment, delivering structural solutions across a range of projects. This role offers the opportunity to work on technically challenging designs while engaging directly with clients and playing a key role in project delivery from enquiry through to completion.

The Role

The Technical Engineer will be responsible for delivering design and technical support across a range of structural projects. Following a period of on-the-job training, you will work independently, liaising directly with customers while producing calculations, drawings and technical solutions to meet project requirements.

You will;

 * Prepare structural calculations and load assessments in line with relevant standards
 * Produce bespoke design solutions and technical drawings using CAD software
 * Generate accurate technical quotations and deliver within agreed timescales
 * Liaise directly with customers, providing technical advice and design support
 * Oversee projects from enquiry through to delivery, ensuring quality and compliance

What's in it for you;

 * Competitive salary and benefits package
 * Flexible working with early finish on a Friday
 * Clear path for career development within a progressive organisation
 * Hands-on role with exposure to design, customer interaction and project delivery

The Ideal Candidate;

 * Degree in an Engineering discipline (e.g. Civil, Structural or Mechanical)
 * Experience working with traditional materials such as timber, steel and concrete
 * Knowledge of AutoCAD and production of manufacturing drawings
 * Strong organisational and communication skills with a customer-focused approach

Full details will be discussed upon application.

Are you a Technical Engineer open to new opportunities? Get in touch, I would love to have a chat!

To speak in absolute confidence about this opportunity please send an up to date CV via the link provided or contact Hannah Liddle, Specialist Consultant at MCS Group 02896935518 .

Even if this position is not right for you, we may have others that are. Please visit MCS Group to view a wide selection of our current jobs www.mcsgroup.jobs

title: HR Coordinator
description:
Great things are happening in the Europe and Australia (EUAU) region. Each day, our 2,500 plus colleagues bring their passion to our offices, manufacturing sites, R&D center, and commercial/distributor markets across Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. We are energized by over 150 years of bringing people together with the food they love. With beloved brands Old El Paso, Häagen-Dazs, Nature Valley, Betty Crocker, Fibre One, Latina Fresh and more, we are on a mission to become the undisputed growth leader in food for EUAU. In a world that’s ever-changing, we look to stay ahead and seek people who share our love of food and are hungry to shape the future.

About The Job

We’re looking for a proactive and organised HR Coordinator to join our UK & Ireland HR team and help deliver an outstanding employee experience across the full colleague lifecycle.

In this role, you’ll coordinate HR activity from offer through onboarding, development, and exit, partnering closely with Talent Acquisition, HR Business Partners, Pay & Benefits, and business leaders to provide seamless support to our teams across the UK & Ireland.

If you enjoy working at the heart of a team, bringing energy to everything you do, and making a real difference to people’s experience at work, this could be a great opportunity for you.

What Your Role Is


 * Coordinate HR processes across the employee lifecycle, including joiners, movers, leavers, and employee changes.
 * Partner with Talent Acquisition, HR Business Partners, Pay & Benefits, and Business Services to deliver a seamless HR service.
 * Act as a first point of contact for operational HR queries, resolving issues or escalating when needed.
 * Manage HR data and processes within our systems, including Workday, ensuring accuracy and efficiency.
 * Collaborate with global and offshore teams to ensure alignment with policies and best practices.
 * Support key HR initiatives across areas such as culture, engagement, wellbeing, diversity & inclusion, and talent development.
   
   

What You'll Bring To The Team

You’re a highly organised self-starter who enjoys seeing tasks through to completion and takes pride in delivering high-quality work. You’re comfortable coordinating across teams, managing multiple priorities, and adapting quickly when plans change.

You will have some experience or knowledge of HR or recruitment through a graduate program or equivalent, but more important is your interest in people, your willingness to learn, and your ability to build strong working relationships. You’re comfortable using tools such as Microsoft Office, and any exposure to Workday would be a bonus.

What’s in it for you?

Work with Heart - offers us flexibility that also requires partnership to ensure we regularly come together in person for those moments that help drive our business forward. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 2 days per week in the office with the opportunity to finish at 15:00 on Fridays.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive, for this we offer free Headspace account for you and up to 5 friends or family members, ongoing activities, Employee Assistance Program and more.

Bonus - Our people are at the heart of what makes General Mills great, so when goals are reached, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Health & Dental Insurances – All colleagues get the opportunity to join our insurances from day one.

Family & Carers Leave - Every family is unique. Our approach allows every family to have the opportunity to spend quality time-off to support them from the point of birth or adoption to care, with enhanced covers.

Enjoy other perks like enhanced pension contributions, subsidised canteen, access to both peer-to-peer recognition and discount platforms, 2 extra days for volunteering work, and more...

Great Place To Work
sector: Manufacturing, Food and Beverage Services, and Food and Beverage Manufacturing
