title: Creative Strategy Design Graduate 2026
description:
What to Expect

We are recruiting for a Graduate to join our world-class Design team, to be based in our Bicester site, in Oxfordshire. As a Creative Strategy Design Graduate, you will work within our Design Studio on cutting-edge projects to add to an existing portfolio of work in automotive design.

This is a two-year rotational programme offering experience across key Design departments within Creative Strategy including CMF design, UI design, Digital Visualisation Design and MSO (McLaren Special Operations) Design. Through four structured rotations, you’ll gain hands-on exposure to McLaren’s Design operations, actively contributing to the continuous improvement of our processes. The programme is designed to build your technical expertise, deepen your operational understanding, and develop your leadership skills.




Please note this is an onsite position.




What You'll Do

During the Graduate Scheme, you’ll gain hands-on experience within McLaren’s Creative Strategy Design operations, contributing to the continuous improvement and excellence of our design processes.




What You'll Bring

 * You must have achieved, or be on track to achieve, at least a 2:1 in a relevant degree.
 * A passion for vehicles and cutting-edge design environments.
 * Strong analytical thinking and structured problem-solving abilities.
 * The drive to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, precision-focused setting.




Personal Attributes

We’re looking for an individual who combines design expertise with curiosity, energy, and commitment to innovation. Desirable traits include:




 * Attention to detail - care and precision in analysis, planning and execution.
 * Continuous improvement thinking - always looking for ways to do things better.
 * Adaptability - able to pivot between technical detail and big-picture thinking.
 * Team collaboration - thrive in cross-functional environments, building trust with operators, engineers and leadership.
 * Resilience - ability to work through challenges and maintain momentum in a high-precision, time-critical environment.
 * Ownership - proactive in taking responsibility and seeing tasks through to completion.
 * Effective communicator - clear and confident when presenting solutions and progress.
 * Passion for performance - enthusiastic about McLaren's vision and products.




What We'll Do for You




We offer a wide-ranging benefits package, which includes:




 * A range of wellbeing initiatives, such as employee assistance programme and free financial & mortgage advice

title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor
description:
Department: Graduate Recruitment

Location: Bristol Water HQ

Compensation: £30,000 / year



Description

Launch your career and become a future leader with our graduate programme at Network Plus!
We recruit high-calibre, motivated graduates and engage with you before you finish University so you can step straight into a graduate role from September 2026, whilst also completing our two-year structured development programme, designed to build and develop your leadership capability whilst also developing specialist skills in your chosen field.

Why choose Network Plus


Network Plus is an award-winning business delivering essential utility and infrastructure services for the UK’s major providers of gas, power, telecoms, transport, water, and wastewater.
We value the variety of experience, perspective, and other points of difference our workforce, clients, and supply chain offer.
We are actively working with colleagues across the Network Plus Group to develop an inclusive environment – we want all our employees to feel valued and included to enable everyone to thrive at work and understand the value of their contribution matters no matter their background, identity, or circumstances.
You’ll complete structured placements across different contracts and business areas, alongside a graduate cohort at the same stage of learning—making rotations and swaps straightforward and impactful and building a strong graduate community.

As a Graduate Quantity Surveyor, you will be at the heart of our commercial team—driving insights, shaping decisions, and helping keep projects on track. You will dive into cost and project data, turn it into meaningful management reports, and deliver weekly performance updates that influence how we operate. You will also play a key role in reviewing and processing subcontractor applications, ensuring our commercial activity runs smoothly and efficiently.

What we offer

Support and Development
 * Dedicated mentor support from our senior management team to guide your journey
 * Leadership development and cohort-based learning resulting in a recognized qualification
 * 360 feedback, coaching and continuous support throughout rotations

Real Impact and Challenge
 * Take part in a Business Sustainability challenge, tackling real-world issues and presenting solutions at an executive level
 * A strong graduate community with peer cohorts where you will grow together throughout the programme

Community
 * Professional skills, confidence and experience that set you up for long‑term success
   

What you’ll be doing
Across your two-year programme you’ll complete four 6-month rotations in different parts of the business, giving you the breadth and depth needed to understand how Network Plus thrives and where your strengths truly lie.
During each rotation, you’ll take on real responsibilities from day one, contributing to live projects and working alongside experienced colleagues who will support your development.


Key Responsibilities


 * Managing and monitoring of work activities carried out against the pre-determined schedule, the number of items supplied and costs
 * Monitoring of staff, labour and plant hire rates utilized on the contract; compiling monthly reports and managing cost allocation
 * Organising and tracking the procurement of sub-contractors
 * Being responsible for the preparation and internal governance authorisation of subcontractor payments
 * Assisting in the placing of subcontract tasks orders in accordance with the main contract
 * Attending operational & subcontractor meetings; monitoring subcontractors against programme
 * Assisting in the preparation of client and subcontract final accounts
 * Ensuring the risk register and change register is kept up to date
 * Supporting the monitoring of internal expenditure against budget/ cost plans and maximise the efficiency of reporting techniques and systems
   
   
   

Experience and Qualifications


 * Curious, driven graduates with a collaborative mindset, a willingness to learn and a desire to make an impact
 * Final‑year students or recent graduates with a role‑related degree (2:2 or above)
 * Full UK driving licence (some travel during placements)
 * Individuals who want responsibility early and enjoy learning by doing
 * Must have graduated within the last two years
 * Negotiation and influencing skills
 * Proficient IT skills, including Microsoft Excel and Word
 * Strong commercial acumen
   
   
   

Salary and Benefits

We offer a competitive salary based on experience along with a full benefits package.

Network Plus is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, nationality, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.

We are Armed Forces-friendly. We welcome applications from ex-Armed Forces personnel, reservists, armed forces veterans, cadet instructors and military spouses/partners.

We understand that privacy and the security of your personal information is extremely important. By applying for this role, you agree to the terms of our privacy policy.

description:
Are you a winner who wants to earn great money and have an immediate impact in the financial services industry?

An incredible role for a motivated, driven and commercially astute graduate to rapidly progress their career within a fast-growing, meritocratic and dynamic finance firm.







(NB: Please do not apply if you consider yourself more of an analyst, as this is a sales role.)

Please note that the employer is unable to provide visa sponsorship.







Why should you apply?




Base Salary: £36,000 plus huge OTE.







Uncapped Earning Potential: You will move into a commission structure where most employees exceed their targets:




 * Year 1 (Fee-earning): £70,000 – £80,000 OTE.
 * Year 2: £85,000 – £120,000 OTE.
 * Year 3: £100,000 – £150,000 OTE.







The Pathway: From Elite Graduate to Industry Leader:




A structured 24-month roadmap featuring four 6-month specialist rotations. You won’t just learn one niche; you’ll gain 360° exposure to the entire commercial finance landscape.







 * Direct "Boardroom" Mentorship: Forget standard HR training. You will receive daily 1-2-1 coaching from Sales Directors and Senior Leaders. Learn from the industry's absolute best.
 * City-Tier Trajectory: Execute high-stakes deals at the UK’s largest privately owned brokerage. This is a London-calibre career with a Northampton lifestyle (and cost of living).
 * Tiered Reward Scaling: Your benefits package evolves as you do. As you hit performance milestones and progress through the programme, you will unlock:
 * Company Car Allowance
 * Private Medical Insurance
 * Subsidised Gym Membership
 * The High-Speed Transition: This is a meritocratic fast-track. By the end of Month 24, you will transition from a raw graduate to a seasoned, high-earning Finance Broker with a six-figure OTE.




The Role:




This is a structured development programme focused on outbound business development. You will:




 * Conduct outbound sales activities to establish rapport with business owners.
 * Qualify client interest through a consultative process to understand their commercial standing.
 * Position financial solutions to help UK SMEs grow and secure new business.







The Person:

 * The Competitive Edge: You are a winner by nature. You’ve played a vital role in a sports team or a competitive society. Have you ever competed at a varsity, regional/national level? Simply: you refuse to be outworked, thriving in environments where there is a clear scoreboard.
 * Elite "Athlete" Mindset: You possess the discipline, resilience, and determined mindset of a high-performing athlete. You don’t get discouraged by a "loss"; you analyse, adapt, and win the next point.
 * Commercial Hunger: You are unapologetically motivated by money and status. You want to be the top biller, the industry leader, and the person your peers are trying to mirror.
 * High Impact Communication: You have the confidence and gravitas to communicate with business owners. You speak with authority and build trust through competence and charisma.
 * The Right Applicant: You don’t settle for average. You have a track record of excellence, demonstrated by being on track for or achieving a 2.1 or above in any degree discipline.







We’d advise NOT applying if:

 * You prefer the comfort zone: This role requires relentless outbound activity and the grit to handle high-stakes negotiations daily.
 * Are not willing to put the work in and go the extra mile: You will operate in a fast-growing, meritocratic environment where rewards are tied to results, not just attendance.
 * You are looking for a desk-bound analyst role: This is a front-line, relationship-building position. If you aren't comfortable picking up the phone and closing deals, this isn't for you.

title: DNA Scientist I
description:
Company Description

Source BioScience Limited is an international provider of integrated state of the art Laboratory Services and Products.

Job Description

Working Hours: 37.5 hours per week, Monday – Friday

The role requires shift work, with core working hours between 9.00 am and midnight.

We are seeking a scientist who, ideally, will have some laboratory experience in molecular biology techniques, and an eye for detail. The successful candidate may be a recent graduate or may have acquired appropriate experience within a laboratory and will understand the principles of confidentiality and data protection. You will be responsible for a wide range of tasks including extracting DNA, processing DNA samples for sequencing, data analysis, as well as participation in other research projects. Candidates will also be expected to cover within other departments within the facility when required.

Applicants should possess good interpersonal skills, practice meticulous record keeping and be conscientious and reliable in their approach to work. DNA sequencing, genotyping/gene expression and robotics experience would be an advantage, as would any experience of working in a service orientated industry.

Summary:

The overall objective is to deliver, either with single responsibility, or in shared projects, services from the portfolio to customers of Source BioScience.

Data is to be delivered promptly, within agreed KPIs, in approved formats whilst keeping meticulously maintained records using LIMS and paper-based formats as appropriate.

The post holder will be expected to perform data analysis.

They will also be expected to undertake general laboratory maintenance and routine tasks including preparation of buffers and reagents, lab tidying etc. The post-holder will be expected to maintain quality standards.

Duties:


 * Perform and adhere to SOP when undertaking laboratory tasks in support of project progression, working independently or in conjunction with other team members.
 * Be able to observe and report problems encountered with possible hardware failure or sample related issues.
 * Teamwork – balancing the different demands and priorities placed upon them by the other members of the group and customers. Working to deadlines and making effective use of the time and resources available
 * Reply to routine enquiries from customers via telephone, email etc and provide technical support to the commercial department
 * Excellent communication skills are required to communicate with colleagues and management, and report to customers in a professional and succinct manner
 * Meticulous nature with a high degree of accuracy is required
 * Self-motivated with a proven good attendance record
 * To respect the confidentiality of all matters that may be learned relating to employment and other members of staff
 * To dress and act in a professional manner as per Company Policy
 * To comply with all quality control procedures to support maintenance of the Source BioScience’s Quality Management System
 * To comply with safe working practices in accordance with Company safety policies
 * To participate in the Annual Joint Staff Review process, in accordance with Company Policy
 * To perform any other duties as may be reasonably required commensurate with grade and experience
 * Attend any training endeavours as appropriate to role or as reasonably requested by Line Manager
   
   

Skills:


 * Good Interpersonal Skills
 * Meticulous Record Keeping
 * Conscientious
 * Reliable
 * Previous sequence/genotyping and robotics experience would be advantageous
   
   

Please do not apply for this position unless you are able to demonstrate the criteria we are seeking within your CV.

Due to the volume of applications, we apologise that we are unable to respond individually to each application. If we have not contacted you within 7 working days your application has been unsuccessful, and your details have not been retained. Please feel free to apply for any other position you may see in the future.

Qualifications

Degree or equivalent experience

location: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description:
Fantastic graduate sales role in Newcastle offering:



· £29,500 basic salary

· Annual profit share

· Market leading company, £370 million turnover

· Fully funded and accredited sales qualification



What do the company do?



Turning over in excess of £370 million, this company are market leading distributor of plumbing, heating and bathroom products. With a phenomenal portfolio of products, they supply to the likes of Screwfix, Wickes, Travis Perkins, Jewson's and many more.



The business also sells products that contain 94% of recycled materials. Their operations are similarly targeted at minimising the company's carbon footprint. Due to expansion, they are looking to hire graduates into their sales team based in Newcastle.



What would my role be?



Working closely with an experienced senior manager mentor, this is an internal sales role that is largely account management focused. You will initially be contacting independent builders and plumbers merchants to introduce new products and upsell where possible. As you develop you will be given more and more responsibility, as your progression routes naturally become more client facing and managing more of the sales process.



You will be immersed in an intensive training period and gain insight into various areas of the business. This will involve product, skills and industry sales training both in-house and with external training providers



What is required for the role?

· Driving license

· Confidence in speaking to new people face-to-face

· Target driven mindset

· A business-minded person

· An excellent communicator

· A drive to succeed



What is the package?



 * Basic salary - £29,500
 * Annual profit sharing scheme
 * 25 days holiday + Bank Holidays
 * Thorough training tailored to the individual
 * Employee discount
 * Life insurance
 * Store discount
 * The opportunity to establish a career with a well-renowned business

Apply today and a member of the team will be in touch to discuss next steps!

title: Graduate Business Development Executive
description:
Graduate Business Development Executive

 * Are you a graduate excited by the idea of software and technology sales?
 * Opportunity to get into a highly lucrative sales industry!
 * Great benefits and company culture such as regular socials, mental health wellness days, team incentives and unlimited annual leave!




As a Graduate Business Development Executive, the package you will receive is:

 * £28,000 basic salary
 * Uncapped commission but expect £37,000+ OTE in year1
 * Fantastic internal training and development scheme
 * Matched pension increased with length of service
 * Unlimited Annual Leave + Birthday off work
 * Laptop and tech stack




What will you be doing?

As a Business Development Executive, you will identify, qualify and book new business opportunities with health and safety professionals. You will look to help win contracts that can exceed £150k. Your role focuses on researching and sourcing potential businesses, so you can proactively target these companies via phone, email and LinkedIn.




Progression is highly encouraged within this company and they promote from within, seeing roles such as Senior BDE, Business Development manager, and Account Executive or a variety or other routes as potential growth opportunities!




Could you be the right fit for this role?

 * You are a graduate of any discipline
 * You want to kick start your career in sales and business development
 * You are a clear communicator, personable, outgoing and professional
 * You have a learning mindset and want to be coached and developed
 * You have a strong work ethic and the grit and stamina to continue when the going gets tough and you face rejection
 * You want to win and be the best, but not at the expense of your team
 * You are based in or commutable to Manchester City Centre




Who are hiring?

Established since the 1990's, our client are a global SaaS company with operations in the US, UK and Australia and are looking to grow their team further with the help of new graduates who are eager and hungry to kick start their sales career. Currently trusted by over 850 customers with 500,000 users worldwide it's an exciting time to join this fast-growing sales team in a company planning for continual growth over the next few years.

title: Associate Consultant
description:
Graduate Opportunity - International Search Consultant - Hedge Fund & Commodities




We are a boutique executive search firm offering impactful work in a small team environment. Prior financial market knowledge is not required - intellectual curiosity is!




Starting in August 2026 and based in our Edinburgh office, we are offering an opportunity to join a rapidly expanding international executive search consultancy. We offer a starting base of £30,000 plus bonus and benefits.




Over the last 10 years HW Anderson have grown into one of the largest global front office buy side search firms. We partner with the world's leading hedge funds and merchant trading platforms to identify, assess and secure investment, trading and analytics talent. H.W. Anderson's unique environment combines the intellectual challenge and compensatory potential of top-tier management consulting with the dynamic, growth-oriented culture of a start-up.




The Firm

At H.W. Anderson your potential is limited only by your ambition. We advise leading businesses on the key strategic challenge of our time: identifying, securing and retaining the best talent in the market.




We're looking for hardworking, intelligent, sociable and competitive problem solvers who are excited about delivering for our clients across the globe.




Candidates that are successful are rewarded with a market-leading training and development program that includes two weeks of intensive instruction and career development tailored to individual interests and aspirations.




You:

Are a graduate (or recent grad) who can demonstrate strong work ethic and intellect.

Are a team player with a competitive edge.

Are a resilient, patient individual who has the mental strength to overcome failure.

Have a natural curiosity and desire to learn about financial markets

Have a great sense of humor and strong social skills.

Have the maturity to deal with senior executives at leading investment firms.

Possess the organizational skills to manage your time effectively in order to multi-task across projects.




The Role

The role of an executive search consultant is to identify exceptional talent that enables our client firms to build or maintain an edge over their competitors in the market. More often than not, our best candidates have to be successfully extracted and incentivized to leave leading platforms. A great search consultant develops the skills necessary to discern the difference between average and exceptional candidates and approaches each individual they engage with both empathy and an honest pragmatism that creates mutually beneficial scenarios for both clients and candidates alike. Developing these skills requires patience and effort. In our entry level consultant role you will be:

 * Working directly alongside our Vice Presidents, Directors and Managing Director's you will be responsible for delivering on a range of mandates across the globe in your position of “Consultant.”
 * Developing and maintaining relationships with key individuals in the market.
 * Creatively map out teams and build out org charts across leading companies.
 * Proactively “head-hunt” senior level executives to generate candidates for your clients.
 * Building subject matter expertise across our core buy side markets.
 * You will work closely with our global offices.




For more information click to see frequently asked questions about the field of executive search. http://www.hw-anderson.com/faq

title: Graduate Technical Administrator
description:
Company:

Finning (UK) Ltd

Number of Openings:

2

Worker Type:

Permanent

Position Overview:

As Graduate Technical Administrator you will be responsible for creating the accurate pricing of machinery repair options for use across the business including EM (Equipment Management) solutions, standard jobs and customer support agreements.

Finning are the largest Caterpillar dealership world-wide, specialising in Heavy Equipment, Machinery, and Power Generation. We have been delivering unrivalled service for 90 years. We sell, rent, supply parts, service, and repair equipment and engines to customers in various industries, including mining, construction, petroleum, forestry, and a wide range of power systems applications.

Job Description:

Major Job Functions


 * Create repair options for components and servicing repairs.
 * Build a price matrix from CAT parts data.
 * Develop an accurate bill of materials, labour hours for repairs and provide any supporting documentation.
 * Provide support to the Contracts Support Supervisor
 * Create complete lifecycle rebuild options for the products we sell and service.
 * Accountable for ensuring that the applications the dealership uses for developing repair options is maintained
 * Promote and gather feedback to improve repair option builds from service operations.
 * Use various reports to maintain accurate repair options accuracy.
 * Provides feedback to ensure accuracy of the repair options are maintained.
   
   

Specific Skills


 * Financial Literacy (numeracy) skills
 * Engineering literacy skills
 * Microsoft Excel (or equivalent) to intermediate/advanced level
   
   

Experience

Essential:


 * Financial Literacy (numeracy) skills
 * Engineering literacy skills
 * Microsoft Excel (or equivalent) to intermediate/advanced level
 * Graduate or 3 years operations experience
   
   

Desirable:


 * Knowledge of plant or industrial equipment preferred.
 * Engineer qualification (Certificate in engineering) or relevant experience in engineering
 * Previous experience in a similar role within similar industry environment
 * Knowledge of OEM systems
 * Good knowledge of Systems and applications desirable
   
   

Accountability


 * Accountable for ensuring that the applications the dealership uses for developing repair options are maintained.
 * Promotes and gathers feedback to improve repair option builds from service operations.
 * Uses various reports to maintain accurate repair options accuracy.
 * Provides feedback to OEM providers to ensure accuracy of the repair options are maintained.
 * Accuracy of Repair Option documentation.
   
   

Education and Experience

Essential:

Graduate or 3 years operations experience

Desirable:

Experience in use of OEM Applications – such as Product Support Software & dealer management systems

Engineer qualification (Certificate in engineering)

Relevant experience in engineering

Previous experience in a similar role within similar industry environment (Construction, HGV, Agricultural equipment, Automotive, Heavy Machinery)

If you are seeking a role that can provide you with your next step in career development and would like to be a part of an industry leading organisation then apply online today!

At Finning, we prioritize creating a diverse and inclusive environment. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we actively encourage all individuals to express themselves and achieve their full potential. As a company, we continuously strive to enhance our outreach to individuals of all backgrounds and identities. We do not discriminate against applicants based on gender identity, race, national and ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation, marital and family status, and/or mental or physical disabilities. Furthermore, Finning is committed to collaborating with and providing reasonable accommodations /adjustments to individuals with disabilities. If you require an adjustment/accommodation at any point during the recruitment process, please inform your recruiter. title: Epidemiological Analyst
description:
London, London (region), NW9 5EQ

Job Summary

The main responsibilities of the postholder will be to develop and maintain enhanced surveillance systems for vaccine preventable diseases, working closely with a wide variety of professionals from both within and out of UKHSA to ensure high quality data. They will understand vaccines, how they work and their utility in the national immunisation programme. They will be proficient at using different database platforms and tools (R, MS Access and SQL) and experienced in using statistical packages for performing epidemiological analyses and writing scientific papers for publication. They will produce regular and ad hoc outputs from these systems for inclusion in routine national and international reports, information requests and peer review articles. They will be comfortable presenting research work at local and national meetings and conferences. They will also manage the disease-specific web pages on the UKHSA website, ensuring that the old content is reviewed and updated in a timely manner. They will be responsible for supporting other departmental staff in website maintenance, developing infectious disease surveillance databases, database querying and report running.

Working for your organisation

We pride ourselves as being an employer of choice, where Everyone Matters promoting equality of opportunity to actively encourage applications from everyone, including groups currently underrepresented in our workforce.  

UKHSA ethos is to be an inclusive organisation for all our staff and stakeholders. To create, nurture and sustain an inclusive culture, where differences drive innovative solutions to meet the needs of our workforce and wider communities. We do this through celebrating and protecting differences by removing barriers and promoting equity and equality of opportunity for all.  

Please visit our careers site for more information https://gov.uk/ukhsa/careers

Job Description


 * Develop and utilise information and data systems related to the projects to underpin public health action across the department and surveillance systems
 * Have responsibility for the development and productivity of the data system(s) related to the projects
 * Undertake specialist and complex analyses of quantitative and/or qualitative datasets
 * Analyse and evaluate quantitative and qualitative data and research evidence from a range of sources to inform decision making
 * Receive, interpret, provide and advise on complex epidemiological and statistical information about the health of populations to the NHS, Department of Health and others
   
   

Specialist Advice and Evaluation


 * Communicate effectively and diplomatically research and other specialist information with colleagues from own or other disciplines and present findings at local, national and international meetings and conferences
 * Write and/or contribute to national and local policy setting reports, as well as peer-reviewed publications in scientific journals, on the health of the population
   
   

Research and Development


 * Undertake literature reviews, perform statistical analysis, evaluative research surveys, audits and other research as required
   
   

Teaching and Training


 * Assist in teaching and training courses run at Centre for Infectious Disease Surveillance and Control, Colindale and outside, by producing presentation materials
   
   

The Postholder Will:


 * Maintain, develop and document enhanced surveillance systems including databases for each area of responsibility in collaboration with Consultant Epidemiologists, Clinical Scientists, Reference laboratory staff, Field service and Health Protection Teams where appropriate
 * Create and update queries and data validation checks for the removal of duplicate records, data formatting and ensuring completeness of data.
 * Liaise with regional stakeholder staff providing surveillance data
 * Liaise with clinicians, laboratories and HPTs regarding individual cases and outbreaks
 * Develop, update and maintain appropriate pages on the UKHSA website and dashboard in a timely manner. Work closely with scientists/managers on validation of content of the web pages to ensure uploaded data meets UKHSA website guidelines
 * Run regular queries on a wide range of databases to prepare annual surveillance reports for Health Protection Report as appropriate.
 * To assist the Information Officer with appropriate ad hoc queries for Parliamentary Questions and other information requests
 * To assist the Information Officer with the preparation of appropriate annual data sets for international organisations eg. World Health Organisation (WHO) and European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC)
 * Maintain appropriate disease specific Reference Manager databases in liaison with departmental scientific and medical staff
 * Contribute to papers for scientific journals and reports for the Department of Health and the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation upon request
 * Present surveillance data and study results at in-house and national meetings/conferences, and attend national meetings/conferences as appropriate
 * Provide database and website support for other infectious disease surveillance projects
 * Undertake any other duties as specified by the line manager, or Head of Department
   
   

PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

You should pursue a programme of continuous professional development in accordance with any relevant professional registration or statutory requirements, while maintaining appropriate awareness of service provider requirements.

Identify, discuss and action own professional performance and training / development needs with your line manager through appraisal / individual development plan. Attending internal / external training events. Be able to share data science practices across departments and in industry, promoting professional development and use of best practice.

To participate in all mandatory training as required, i.e. fire safety, information governance and all other mandatory training.

KEY WORKING RELATIONSHIPS

The post holder will develop working relationships and communicate regularly with a wide range of individuals, clinical and non-clinical, both internal and external to UKHSA. This will include;

Internal


 * Vaccine coverage and vaccine safety and effectiveness staff
 * Immunisation Division staff
 * Colleagues in appropriate Reference Laboratories, Data and Information Systems staff
 * ICT and Digital staff
 * Public Health Data Science staff
 * Local and Regional Services and Health Protection teams
   
   

External


 * NHS laboratories (including medical, scientific, information and administrative staff)
 * NHS Digital, NHS England and NHS Improvement, DHSC, NIMS
 * Child Health Records Department managers, Screening, and Immunisation teams, PHEC staff, Directors of Public Health, Immunisation Co-ordinators and immunisation leads
 * Other Public Health Agencies in the United Kingdom
   
   

Please note this list is not exhaustive

Person specification

Essential Role Criteria


 * Educated to degree level (or equivalent level of qualification) in a relevant science subject
 * Post-graduate (MSc) degree in Epidemiology
 * Experience of scientific research, particularly in public health or epidemiology
 * Data management, analysis and reporting of epidemiological data using R, SQL and Microsoft Access
 * Preparing scientific reports and/or presentations
 * Able to work as a team member
 * Able to work independently and to deadlines
 * Maintain required levels of confidentiality
 * Able to communicate and document methods clearly and succinctly
 * Able to liaise with a wide range of external collaborators including health service and academic professionals
 * An understanding of and commitment to equality of opportunity and good working relationships
   
   

Desirable role criteria


 * Experience working within health protection
   
   

Alongside your salary of £37,749, UK Health Security Agency contributes £10,935 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides.


 * Learning and development tailored to your role
 * An environment with flexible working options
 * A culture encouraging inclusion and diversity
 * A Civil Service pension with an employer contribution of 28.97%
   
   

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Selection process details

This vacancy will be assessed using a competency-based framework which will assess your qualifications, knowledge and experience and / or skills and abilities outlined in the essential criteria.

Stage 1: Application & Sift

Competency Based

You Will Be Required To Complete An Application Form. You Will Be Assessed On The Listed 11 Essential Criteria, And This Will Be In The Form Of A:


 * Application form (‘Employer/ Activity history’ section on the application)
 * 750 word supporting statement.
   
   

Healthjobs UK has a word limit of 1500, but your supporting statement must be no more than 750 words. Please do not exceed 750 words.

This should outline how you consider your skills, experience and knowledge provide evidence of your suitability for the role, with reference to the essential criteria.

You will receive a joint score for your application form and statement. (The application form is the kind of information you would put into your CV – please note you will not be able to upload your CV. Please complete the application form in as much detail as possible). Please do not email us your CV.

Longlisting: In the event of a large number of applications we will longlist into 3 piles of:


 * Meets all essential criteria
 * Meets some essential criteria
 * Meets no essential criteria
   
   

We will take pile ‘Meets all essential criteria’ will proceed to shortlisting. Feedback will not be provided at this stage.

Shortlisting: In the event of a large number of applications we will conduct an initial sift, on the lead criteria of:


 * Post-graduate (MSc) degree in Epidemiology
 * Experience of scientific research, particularly in public health or epidemiology
 * Data management, analysis and reporting of epidemiological data using R, SQL and Microsoft Access
   
   

Desirable criteria may be used in the event of a large number of applications/large amount of successful candidates.

If you are successful at this stage, you will progress to interview & assessment.

Feedback will not be provided at this stage.

Stage 2: Interview

Competency Based

You will be invited to a single face to face interview at our site in Colindale. In exceptional circumstances, we may be able to offer a remote interview.

Knowledge, experience, skills & abilities will be tested at interview.

There will be a one-hour technical assessment prior to the interview (on the same day) and the first part of the interview will be based off of the assessment followed by more generic questions, leaving time for you at the end to ask us questions. The assessment will be a short coding assessment in R. A dataset will be provided for analysis, and candidates will be asked to demonstrate their ability to write clear, structured code to explore the data. The exercise is designed to assess epidemiological thinking and coding approach, rather than arriving at the “correct” answers. We are primarily interested in how candidates structure their analysis, interpret data, and communicate their reasoning through code.

Interviews will be held on the 15th and 16th April. Please note, these dates are subject to change.

Once this job has closed, the job advert will no longer be available. You may want to save a copy for your records.

Additional Information

Location

This role is being offered as office based at our Scientific Campus in Colindale. Based at our scientific campus, you will be required to have a Counter Terrorism Check security vetting check as a minimum. For meaningful National Security Vetting checks to be carried out individuals need to have lived in the UK for a sufficient period of time. For meaningful national security vetting checks to be carried out individuals need to have lived in the United Kingdom for a sufficient period of time. You should normally have been resident in the UK for the last 3 years as the role requires CTC clearance. UK residency less than the outlined periods may not necessarily bar you from gaining national security vetting and applicants should contact the Vacancy Holder / Recruiting Manager listed in the advert for further advice.

Security Clearance Level Requirement

Successful candidates must pass a basic disclosure and barring security check before they can be appointed.

Successful candidates must meet the security requirements before they can be appointed. The level of security needed is Counter-Terrorist Check.

Eligibility Criteria

Open to all external applicants (anyone) from outside the Civil Service (including internal applicants).

Salary Information

If you are successful at interview, and are moving from another government department, NHS, or Local Authority, the relevant starting salary principles for level transfers or promotions will apply. Otherwise, roles are offered at the pay scale minimum for the grade, but in exceptional circumstances there may be flexibility if you are able to demonstrate you are already in receipt of an existing, higher salary. Pay increases are through the relevant annual pay award for the role and terms.

Please be aware that the salary is based on the office location.

Future location

UKHSA is investing in a new state-of-the-art National Biosecurity Centre in Harlow, Essex, which will eventually bring together teams currently based at Canary Wharf, Colindale and Porton Down. For more details, please see: Huge biosecurity centre investment to boost pandemic protection - GOV.UK.

The new facilities will start becoming operational in the mid-2030s, with full completion by 2038. Staff will move in phases as facilities become available. If you're appointed to a role currently based at Canary Wharf, Colindale or Porton Down, please note that we'll continue investing in these sites for the next decade. As we get closer to the transition, we'll provide full information about relocation support available to staff.

Reasonable Adjustments

The Civil Service is committed to making sure that our selection methods are fair to everyone. To help you during the recruitment process, we will consider any reasonable adjustments that could help you. An adjustment is a change to the recruitment process or an adjustment at work. This is separate to the Disability Confident Scheme. If you need an adjustment to be made at any point during the recruitment process you should contact the recruitment team in confidence as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

You can find out more information about reasonable adjustments across the Civil Service here: https://www.civil-service-careers.gov.uk/reasonable-adjustments

International Police check

title: Graduate Test Engineer
description:
Graduate Test Engineer

Location: London

Starting Salary: £28,000

Application Requirements

 * Minimum 2:1 degree
 * Right to work in the UK unsponsored for the duration of the programme
 * Ability to work on site, five days per week
 * This role requires a high level of security clearance. We therefore require applicants to have been based in the UK for the last five years as a minimum.




Grayce is not on the UK Border Agency's Sponsor Register and is unable to sponsor work visas.




Why Grayce?

Grayce specialises in delivering change and transformation for some of the UK’s most ambitious organisations. For over a decade, we have partnered with FTSE 100 and FTSE 250 companies to deploy high performing early career talent across major programmes of work.

Our Accelerated Development Programme offers a structured, fast track route into industry. You will gain hands on experience with leading organisations while developing professional skills, earning industry recognised qualifications, and receiving dedicated coaching and mentoring throughout your journey.




This role sits within the Grayce Technology Pathway and forms part of our wider Technology Graduate intake.




Graduate Test Engineer

As a Graduate Test Engineer, you will play a key role in ensuring the quality, reliability and performance of critical software systems that support high‑profile public‑sector services. Working within a structured, secure environment, you will support test planning, test execution and analysis across both functional and non‑functional aspects of complex applications.

You will contribute to risk‑based test planning, support the creation and refinement of test scripts, investigate defects, and collaborate closely with software engineers to ensure common understanding of expected system behaviour. You will also work with business analysts to confirm that key business scenarios are accurately captured within acceptance criteria.

This role provides exposure to modern testing practices used by major global technology consultancies - giving you insight into engineering excellence, enterprise‑scale delivery and quality assurance in highly regulated environments. It is well‑suited to individuals who demonstrate strong analytical thinking, attention to detail and a methodical approach to problem‑solving.




What Makes a Great Grayce Analyst

Academic Background

 * A minimum 2:1 degree

Technical Skills (advantageous)

 * An interest in building capability in areas such as:
 * Functional and non-functional testing
 * Defect analysis and quality assurance practices
 * BDD approaches and tools such as Cucumber
 * Programming fundamentals in Java, Python or similar
 * Agile delivery tools such as Jira and Confluence
 * Performance testing concepts




Professional Skills

Strong communication, structured thinking, stakeholder engagement, organisation and a commitment to continuous learning.

Why Work for Us?

Opportunity

Hands on experience working full time onsite with leading organisations, supported by accredited training and industry aligned development.

Delivery

You will typically work with one client during your time at Grayce, delivering high quality outcomes in a structured, professional environment.

Mentoring

Dedicated coaching and support throughout your placement and development journey.

Progression

Completion of a minimum of six industry recognised accreditations, with clear pathways for progression and salary growth.

Wellbeing Support

24/7 access to our Employee Assistance Programme, offering confidential financial, legal, health and wellbeing support.

title: Product Development & Compliance Assistant
description:
About the Role

Santoro is an award-winning design house which creates and distributes a broad variety of gift, stationery and lifestyle collections including 3D pop-up greetings cards, character brands and luxury interiors lines. We are seeking a highly organised and detail-oriented Product Development & Compliance Assistant to support our product lifecycle processes — taking concept development through regulatory compliance processes to a successful market launch.




This role combines hands-on product support with administrative and regulatory responsibilities. You will help ensure our products not only meet performance and quality standards but also comply with all relevant legislation, certifications, and international trade requirements.




This role would suit either:

 * An experienced product development or compliance professional looking for a diverse and challenging role, or
 * A bright, motivated graduate with strong organisational skills and initiative who is keen to build a career in product development, purchasing and compliance.

We are particularly interested in individuals who are hard-working, resilient and engaged, who will relish the challenge of working across a critical path full of a variety of demanding projects. This is a full-time on-site role at our Wimbledon HQ in South London.




Key Responsibilities

Product Development Support

 * Assist in turning creative concepts into fully realised stationery and gift products
 * Track multiple projects simultaneously, monitoring cost, schedule, and quality requirements
 * Support concept development and supplier sourcing activities
 * Coordinate new product development projects with international suppliers
 * Conduct sample quality control checks and document required improvements.
 * Manage and monitor production schedules to ensure timely delivery
 * Liaise with suppliers to obtain technical data, samples, and declarations
 * Set packaging briefs to meet legislative labelling requirements and cross check conformity
 * Maintain product specifications and technical files
 * Drive improvements in the use of materials to improve sustainability
 * Provide ad hoc support to our product development, marketing and sales teams

 

Compliance & Regulatory Support

 * Maintain compliance documentation including declarations of conformity, technical files, and risk assessments to fulfil obligations for traceability and demonstrate due diligence
 * Assist in ensuring products meet applicable UK/EU/USA regulatory standards and directives
 * Coordinate product testing with accredited external laboratories where required
 * Monitor and research regulatory updates and communicate changes to relevant teams
 * Maintain certification records and support customer enquiries
 * Compile, review and monitor supplier audit records to aid selection of reliable partners




Customs & Trade Support

 * Coordinate with customs brokers and customers to provide compliance documentation for exports
 * Research and monitor legislative requirements for new regulations / markets / customers
 * Support fulfilment of FOB orders and special customer needs, including processing manuals, communicating instructions, and managing outcomes




Role Requirements

 * Top grades throughout education including a high degree grade in a relevant subject
 * Ideally 2+ years’ experience in a relevant role but open to ambitious graduate applications
 * Strong organisational skills and a meticulous eye for detail
 * Ability to work accurately and efficiently under pressure
 * Capable of analysing and presenting technical information clearly and concisely
 * Great interpersonal skills and a clear, concise communication style
 * Proactive, logical and solution-focused mindset
 * The ambition and confidence to develop a specialism within this role
 * A good level of proficiency in Microsoft Office programs
 * Good understanding of regulatory frameworks and product standards (desirable)
 * Familiarity with import/export documentation processes (desirable)




Benefits

 * On-site parking
 * Supportive and collaborative working environment
 * Opportunities for career progression
 * Casual dress
 * Company events
 * Employee discount




Salary: £27,000 - £35,000pa DOE.

title: Domestic Assistant
description:

At HC-One, kindness is more than a word. It is a belief that caring for people means caring about what matters most to them so we can support them to lead their best possible life.

To be able to provide the kindest possible care there are 5 values or qualities that everyone who is part of the HC-One team shows up with, supports and coaches in each other every single day. We call these 5 qualities Kindness the HC-One Way: We are curious and ask questions, look for clues and seek to understand. We are compassionate and try to walk in the shoes of others. We're creative yet practical, using our imagination to make things happen. We are courageous in how we support residents to live the life they want, and we can always be counted on.

We champion inclusivity, fostering a workplace where everyone feels valued and can be themselves. Our diverse and inclusive workforce allows us to learn from each other and better represent the communities we serve.

We want you to have an amazing experience working at HC-One, starting with our commitment to providing the quickest possible turnaround from the point of making you a job offer to you starting with us on day one.

title: Graduate - Human Resources Generalist
description:
Are you looking for a unique opportunity to be a part of something great? Want to join a 17,000-member team that works on the technology that powers the world around us? Looking for an atmosphere of trust, empowerment, respect, diversity, and communication? How about an opportunity to own a piece of a multi-billion dollar (with a B!) global organization? We offer all that and more at Microchip Technology Inc.

People come to work at Microchip because we help design the technology that runs the world. They stay because our culture supports their growth and stability. They are challenged and driven by an incredible array of products and solutions with unlimited career potential. Microchip’s nationally-recognized Leadership Passage Programs support career growth where we proudly enroll over a thousand people annually. We take pride in our commitment to employee development, values-based decision making, and strong sense of community, driven by our Vision, Mission, and 11 Guiding Values; we affectionately refer to it as the Aggregate System and it’s won us countless awards for diversity and workplace excellence.

Our company is built by dedicated team players who love to challenge the status quo; we did not achieve record revenue and over 30 years of quarterly profitability without a great team dedicated to empowering innovation. People like you.

Visit our careers page to see what exciting opportunities and company perks await!

Job Description

We are seeking a motivated and detail‑oriented HR Generalist, ideally a recent graduate, looking to develop their career within a fast‑paced and supportive HR environment. This role provides broad exposure across the full HR lifecycle and the opportunity to work closely with HR Managers and stakeholders across our UK and European sites.

Reporting to the Senior HR Manager, this is a full time role and will be located at our Manufacturing site based in Caldicot, South Wales.

Due to the nature of the role and the need to support a Manufacturing environment the role will be site based.

About The Role

As an HR Generalist, you will support day‑to‑day HR operations while contributing to a variety of people‑focused initiatives. Your responsibilities will span recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, policy implementation, absence management, and HR system administration. You will play a key role in ensuring accurate employee data, delivering high‑quality HR support, and helping maintain a positive employee experience throughout the organisation.

Key Responsibilities


 * Maintain, update, and audit employee records in Workday; prepare routine and ad‑hoc HR metrics and reports.
 * Support absence management processes, including long‑term sickness and family leave.
 * Manage and track right‑to‑work documentation in line with legislation.
 * Respond to queries regarding benefits, employee information, and compensation.
 * Support the development, implementation, and communication of HR policies and procedures.
 * Assist HR Managers with employee relations activities, including data gathering, investigations, and report preparation.
 * Support recruitment for permanent, graduate, and temporary roles, including screening and interviews.
 * Prepare HR documentation such as offer letters, leaver letters, and meeting invitations.
 * Deliver onboarding and induction sessions for new starters and coordinate the leaver process.
 * Contribute to HR projects and build strong working relationships across the organisation.
 * The successful candidate will have the ability to handle sensitive information with confidentiality and professionalism
   
   

Requirements/Qualifications


 * Recent graduate with a degree in HR or a business‑related field.
 * Excellent organisational and time‑management skills, with the ability to prioritise effectively.
 * Strong attention to detail and accuracy when managing high‑volume work.
 * Self‑motivated, proactive, and eager to learn and progress.
 * Confident communicator with the ability to influence at all levels.
 * Sound understanding of employment legislation.
 * Strong Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel.
   
   

Travel Time

Why Grayce?

Grayce specialises in developing high-performing early-career talent and placing them into impactful roles across the UK. For over a decade, we’ve partnered with FTSE 100 and 250 organisations to deliver meaningful change.

As part of our Development Programme, you’ll gain hands-on client experience, receive tailored learning, and work towards industry-recognised certifications. With a focus on capability-building, you’ll be supported to grow into roles across Service Management, IT Operations, PMO and wider change functions.
